title: Explicit legal-state gate for dry vs wet-exempt strategy
summary: FIA rules make the mandatory two-dry-compound obligation disappear once a driver has used intermediate or wet tyres.
rationale: FIA rules make the mandatory two-dry-compound obligation disappear once a driver has used intermediate or wet tyres. Current best solutions already track slick counts and wet flags, but not the full legality state, so they may mix compulsory-stop rows with purely tactical rows.
implementation_hint: Create race-driver state features such as `wet_exempt`, `dry_noncompliant_one_slick_used`, `dry_compliant`, `current_tyre_can_finish_but_owes_compound_change`, and interact them with `LapsRemaining`, `TyreLife`, `Stint`, and prior pit count. Test either a gating feature set inside one model or separate specialists for dry-legal vs wet/mixed sessions.
expected_effect: Should reduce late-race confusion by distinguishing rows where a stop is forced by rules from rows where the stop is optional.
risk: Wet or mixed-condition rows may be sparse, so hard gating could fragment the data too much if not regularized.
